supply_chain.dependencies: Some critical parts, components and subassemblies are designed by KLA and manufactured by suppliers in accordance with specifications, while other parts are standard commercial products. Certain parts and raw materials may be obtained only from a single supplier or a limited group of suppliers. Partial or complete loss of certain sources could disrupt scheduled deliveries to customers.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to supplier financial difficulties, geopolitical tensions, natural disasters, pandemics, increased lead times, component shortages, and concentration of suppliers. Tariffs and trade restrictions may increase costs and disrupt supply chain. Rare earth mineral export controls by China may impact availability of critical components.

regulatory_risks: U.S. export controls and BIS Rules restricting sales to China-based entities on U.S. Entity List
Export licensing requirements for China-based customers that are military end users or engaged in military end uses
Commerce Department restrictions on semiconductor capital equipment exports to China for advanced node IC production
Tariffs imposed by U.S. government on imports and reciprocal tariffs by other countries
Section 232 investigations into effects on U.S. national security of imports of semiconductors and semiconductor manufacturing equipment
Environmental laws and regulations including restrictions on PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances)
Climate change and GHG emissions regulations in European Union, California and other jurisdictions
Data privacy requirements and evolving regulations
Economic sanctions and export control regulations
Conflict minerals and responsible sourcing requirements
Tax law changes including GILTI/NCTI provisions, CAMT (corporate alternative minimum tax), and Pillar Two global minimum tax
California State Budget provisions suspending use of NOLs and limiting R&D tax credits
Securities laws and regulations including SEC disclosure requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions against Russia, Belarus and certain regions/entities due to war between Russia and Ukraine
U.S. Entity List restrictions preventing sales to certain China-based entities without export licenses
Export controls on China-based customers for military end uses
Potential additional sanctions, export controls or other measures by U.S., European Union and other countries
Chinese government retaliatory regulations in response to U.S. government actions
Restrictions on rare earth mineral exports by China impacting supply chain
